---
title: "SC-IM Spreadsheets in Terminal"
title-block-style: none
subtitle: "Master terminal-based spreadsheets in just 1 hour"
description: "Learn SC-IM, a powerful spreadsheet program that runs in the terminal, offering a lightweight alternative to traditional spreadsheet applications."
page-layout: custom
toc: false
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .m { --c: #F5A623; }
        .ml-content { max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 1.5rem; }
        .ml-content h1, .ml-content h2 { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-top: 3rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; padding-bottom: 0.75rem; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06); }
        [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-content h1, [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-content h2 { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }
        .ml-content h3 { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 600; margin-top: 2rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
        .ml-content h1:first-child, .ml-content h2:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
        .ml-content p { color: var(--g, #86868b); line-height: 1.7; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
        .ml-content ul, .ml-content ol { color: var(--g, #86868b); line-height: 1.8; padding-left: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
        .ml-content li { padding: 0.2rem 0; }
        .ml-content strong { color: inherit; font-weight: 600; }
        .ml-meta { display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 2rem; }
        .ml-meta-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--g, #86868b); }
        .ml-meta-item i { color: var(--c); }
        .ml-card { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.02); border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06); border-radius: 16px; padding: 2rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
        [data-bs-theme="dark"] .ml-card { background: rgba(245,166,35,0.04); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); }
        .ml-card h3, .ml-card h4 { margin-top: 0; border: none; padding-bottom: 0; }
        .ml-related { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 1.25rem; }
        @media (max-width: 767px) { .ml-related { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }
        </style>
date-modified: last-modified
categories:
  - spreadsheets
  - terminal
  - data-analysis
  - productivity
---

```{=html}
<div class="m">

<div class="ev-hero" style="min-height:50vh;">
  <div class="ev-hero-bg" style="background-image:url('/assets/images/stock/terminal-green.webp');"></div>
  <div class="ev-hero-content">
    <div class="ev-hero-tag" style="background:#F5A623;">Microlearning</div>
    <h1>SC-IM Spreadsheets in Terminal</h1>
    <p>Terminal-based spreadsheet power for data analysis</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.ml-content}

## Session Overview

This focused 1-hour session introduces SC-IM (Spreadsheet Calculator Improved), a powerful terminal-based spreadsheet application. You'll learn how to navigate, manipulate data, create formulas, and perform data analysis tasks entirely within a text-based environment. This lightweight but powerful alternative to traditional GUI spreadsheet applications offers speed, efficiency, and the ability to integrate with other command-line tools.

### Next Session

- **Date:** June 15, 2026
- **Time:** 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM
- **Format:** Available in-person or live virtual
- **Prerequisites:** Basic command line familiarity
- **Price:** $39 per participant (includes reference materials and sample spreadsheets)

## Session Curriculum

### Part 1: SC-IM Basics (15 minutes)

**Introduction to SC-IM**
- History and philosophy of terminal-based spreadsheets
- Installation and initial setup
- Interface overview and layout
- Navigation and movement commands
- Basic cell manipulation
- Hands-on exercise: Creating your first SC-IM spreadsheet

### Part 2: Data Entry and Formatting (15 minutes)

**Working with Data**
- Entering and editing cell data
- Number formats and text alignment
- Cell formatting options
- Working with columns and rows
- Copying and moving data
- Hands-on exercise: Formatting a data table

### Part 3: Formulas and Functions (15 minutes)

**Calculations and Analysis**
- Basic formula syntax
- Built-in functions overview
- Cell references and ranges
- Mathematical operations
- Text manipulation functions
- Hands-on exercise: Creating formulas for data analysis

### Part 4: Advanced Features and Integration (15 minutes)

**Power User Techniques**
- Importing and exporting data
- CSV and Excel compatibility
- Keyboard shortcuts for efficiency
- Integrating with command line tools
- Automation possibilities
- Hands-on exercise: Creating a data workflow with SC-IM

## Learning Outcomes

By completing this microlearning session, you will be able to:

- Navigate and edit data in the SC-IM interface
- Format cells, rows, and columns efficiently
- Create and use formulas for calculations
- Import data from CSV and other formats
- Export results for use in other applications
- Understand the advantages of terminal-based spreadsheets
- Incorporate SC-IM into command-line workflows

## Teaching Methods

```{=html}
<div class="row mt-4">
  <div class="col-md-6">
    <div class="card mb-4">
      <div class="card-header bg-primary text-white">
        <h3 class="card-title mb-0">Hands-On Practice</h3>
      </div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <p>This session emphasizes learning by doing:</p>
        <ul>
          <li>Step-by-step guided exercises</li>
          <li>Real-world data examples</li>
          <li>Interactive command practice</li>
          <li>Problem-solving challenges</li>
          <li>Practical application scenarios</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="col-md-6">
    <div class="card mb-4">
      <div class="card-header bg-primary text-white">
        <h3 class="card-title mb-0">Visual Learning</h3>
      </div>
      <div class="card-body">
        <p>Despite being terminal-based, we use visualization to aid learning:</p>
        <ul>
          <li>Command and shortcut reference charts</li>
          <li>Workflow diagrams</li>
          <li>Live demonstrations</li>
          <li>Before/after comparisons</li>
          <li>Visual command mapping</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Who Should Attend

This microlearning session is ideal for:

- Command line enthusiasts seeking spreadsheet functionality
- Data analysts who work frequently in terminal environments
- System administrators who need quick data manipulation tools
- Developers looking to incorporate spreadsheet functionality in scripts
- Anyone interested in lightweight alternatives to Excel/LibreOffice

Basic familiarity with the command line is recommended but not required.

## Materials and Resources

Participants will receive:

- SC-IM command reference guide
- Sample spreadsheets for practice
- Data sets for analysis exercises
- Integration examples with other tools
- Shortcut cheat sheet for rapid operation
- 30 days of email support for SC-IM questions

:::

```{=html}
<div class="m-s">
  <div class="ml-content">
    <div class="ml-card">
      <h3>Your Instructor</h3>
      <p><strong>Laura Chen</strong> — Data Analyst, chen.ist</p>
      <p>Laura specializes in command line data analysis tools and has been using SC-IM for over 5 years in professional data analysis workflows. She has developed numerous templates and automation scripts that leverage SC-IM's power for data processing tasks. With a background in both statistics and computer science, Laura excels at explaining technical concepts in accessible ways, helping users see the practical benefits of terminal-based data tools.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="ml-card">
      <h3>What Participants Say</h3>
      <p><em>"I never thought I could do spreadsheet work in the terminal until this session. SC-IM has completely changed how I handle data analysis tasks - it's faster, more efficient, and integrates perfectly with my other command line tools."</em></p>
      <p><strong>— Michael R., Systems Administrator</strong></p>
    </div>
    <div class="ml-card">
      <p><em>"As someone who works with data daily, discovering SC-IM through this session was a game-changer. The hands-on format got me productive quickly, and I was able to implement what I learned immediately in my workflow."</em></p>
      <p><strong>— Elena D., Data Analyst</strong></p>
    </div>
    <h3>Related Offerings</h3>
    <div class="ml-related">
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>Unix Command Line Introduction</h4>
        <p>Build a foundation in command line skills</p>
        <a href="/academy/microlearning/cli-intro/" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>Tmux Terminal Multiplexer</h4>
        <p>Enhance your terminal productivity</p>
        <a href="/academy/microlearning/tmux/" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
      <div class="ml-card">
        <h4>LibreOffice Calc</h4>
        <p>GUI-based alternative for spreadsheet users</p>
        <a href="/academy/microlearning/libreoffice-calc/" class="m-bo">Learn More</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="m-cta">
  <div class="container">
    <h2>SC-IM Spreadsheets in Terminal</h2>
    <p>Interested in this program? Get in touch to learn more.</p>
    <a href="/contact.html" class="m-lk">Contact Us</a>
  </div>
</div>

</div>
```
